UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, DEFENDANT JEFFREY APPENRODT MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT, PARAGRAPH 50 IS STRICKEN WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. AS A GENERAL RULE DAMAGES FOR MENTAL SUFFERING ARE NOT AVAILABLE IN CONTRACT CAUSES OF ACTION. SAYWER V. BANK OF AMERICA (1978) 83 CAL.APP.3D 135. PLAINITFF CITES NO CASES TO SUPPORT AN EXCEPTION FROM THE GENERAL RULE UNDER THE CIRCUMSTANCES OF THIS CASE. PARAGRAPH 55 IS STRICKEN WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGE IN GOOD FAITH CONSPIRACY LIABILITY.. COURT GRANTS PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST TO FILE AMENDED COMPLAINT IN TWENTY DAYS. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: M. OLOPERNES, NOT REPORTED

UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, DEFENDANT JEFFREY APPENRODT'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED AS FOLLOWS: DEMURER TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INFECTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. NO OPPOSITION FILED. DEMURER TO THE FIRST AND EIGHT CAUSES OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF SFRO AND FRAUD ARE SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE EACH ELEMENT OF EACH CAUSE OF ACTION AS TO THE MOVING DEFENDANT OR TO SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGE CONSPIRACY LIABILITY. COURT GRANTS PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST TO FILE AMENDED COMPLAINT IN TWENTY DAYS. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: M. REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT JEFFREY APPENRODT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AND OF MOVING PARTY'S INTENTION TO MOVE POST HEARING ON THIS MOTION FOR AN AWARD OF CONTRACTUAL ATTORNEYS FEES IS DENIED. MOVING PARTY FAILED TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1350(B). "IF SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS SOUGHT, WHETHER SEPARATELY OR AS AN ALTERNATIVE TO THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, THE SPECIFIC CAUSE OF ACTION, AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE, CLAIMS FOR DAMAGES, OR ISSUES OF DUTY MUST BE STATED SPECIFICALLY IN THE NOTICE OF MOTION AND BE REPEATED, VERBATIM, IN THE SEPARATE STATEMENT OF UNDISPUTED MATERIAL FACTS." PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: M. OLOPERNES, NOT REPORTED

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION And Of Moving Party'S Intention To Move Post Hearing On This Motion For An Award Of Contractual Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Jun-26-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
James D. Biernat, Esq. (650-689-5160), for Jeffrey Appenrodt, Defendant and moving party.
Stuart R. Curran, Esq. (415-509-7720), for Gregory Hanson, Plaintiff and opposing party.

Plaintiff submits to the tentative ruling. Defendant argues that 1st issue was verbatim and 2nd issue was abbreviated. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT JEFFREY APPENRODT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION and of Moving Party's Intention To Move Post Hearing On This Motion For An Award Of Contractual Attorneys Fees is DENIED. Moving party failed to comply with CRC 3.1350(b). "If summary adjudication is sought, whether separately or as an alternative to the motion for summary judgment, the specific cause of action, affirmative defense, claims for damages, or issues of duty must be stated specifically in the notice of motion and be repeated, verbatim, in the separate statement of undisputed material facts." Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not Reported, D501.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-14-2014 Continued To Feb-04-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-16-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Stuart R. Curran, Esq., (415-509-7720), for Gregory Hanson, Plaintiff and opposing party.
No appearance by Defendant and moving party.

Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT JEFFREY APPENRODT MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT, Paragraph 50 is stricken without leave to amend. As a general rule damages for mental suffering are not available in contract causes of action. Saywer v. Bank of America (1978) 83 Cal.App.3d 135. Plainitff cites no cases to support an exception from the general rule under the circumstances of this case. Paragraph 55 is stricken with leave to amend to sufficiently allege in good faith conspiracy liability.. Court grants plaintiff's request to file amended complaint in twenty days. Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not Reported, D501.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-16-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Stuart R. Curran, Esq., (415-509-7720), for Gregory Hanson, Plaintiff and opposing party.
No appearance by Defendant and moving party.

Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT JEFFREY APPENRODT's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained as follows: Demurer to the fourth cause of action for intentional infection of emotional distress is sustained without leave to amend. No opposition filed.
Demurer to the first and eight causes of action for violation of SFRO and fraud are sustained with leave to amend to allege each element of each cause of action as to the moving defendant or to sufficiently allege conspiracy liability. Court grants plaintiff's request to file amended complaint in twenty days. Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not Reported, D501.
